title: Orbit Systems
compiled_truth:
Orbit Systems is a supply chain visibility platform headquartered in Rotterdam, with engineering offices in Atlanta and Singapore. Founded in 2021 by former freight-forwarding operators, the company built its reputation on real-time shipment tracking and predictive ETA modeling for mid-market logistics providers. By 2026, Orbit had grown to roughly 400 employees and was processing telemetry from over 120,000 connected assets globally — containers, trucks, warehouse sensors, the works.

Orbit's CTO, Daan Kuipers, had been vocal internally about the fragility of their data pipeline. Sensor payloads were getting larger, authentication overhead was eating into latency budgets, and their existing compression stack required multiple passes that introduced drift in time-sensitive corridors. When [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) presented at a logistics tech showcase in Amsterdam in mid-2026, Kuipers reportedly pulled one of the founders aside and asked if they could run a proof-of-concept against Orbit's Southeast Asia corridor data. That POC turned into a pilot, and the pilot turned into Anchor's first six-figure contract, signed in Q4 2026.

The deal was significant for both sides. For Anchor, it validated single-pass primitives in a high-throughput production environment outside of synthetic benchmarks. For Orbit, the integration reportedly cut their per-message processing cost by nearly 40% on the affected corridors — a number that got whispered around logistics circles and drew attention from larger players.

Orbit's VP of Partnerships, Lena Osei, drove much of the commercial negotiation. She pushed hard for exclusivity in the logistics vertical, which Anchor ultimately declined, though the final contract did include a 90-day head start clause before Anchor could onboard direct competitors. Internal memos suggest Lena saw deterministic infrastructure as a potential moat for Orbit's premium tier offering.

By mid-2027, Orbit had expanded the integration beyond the initial corridor to cover their entire Asia-Pacific footprint. There was serious internal discussion about acquring [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) outright — Kuipers apparently championed it at the board level — though as of late 2027 those conversations seem to have cooled, likely due to Orbit's own capital constraints following a delayed Series D. Orbit remains one of Anchor's most strategically important customers and a frequent reference account in enterprise sales cycles.
timeline:
- **2026-06-12** | Daan Kuipers attends logistics tech showcase in Amsterdam; first contact with [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) founding team.
- **2026-07-30** | POC kicked off using Orbit's Southeast Asia corridor telemetry data. Initial results promising.
- **2026-09-18** | Pilot phase begins; Orbit deploys Anchor primitives on a subset of container tracking payloads.
- **2026-11-22** | Six-figure contract signed — Anchor's first major enterprise deal. Lena Osei and Kuipers both sign off.
- **2027-01-10** | Integration goes live across three SEA shipping lanes. Latency improvements confirmed in production.
- **2027-04-05** | Orbit references Anchor integration in a partner webinar, generating inbound interest from two competing logistics platforms.
- **2027-07-19** | Expansion phase: Orbit rolls out [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor-codec) integration across full APAC footprint.
- **2027-09-02** | Internal Orbit board discussion about potential acquisition of Anchor. Kuipers presents strategic rationale.
- **2027-12-11** | Orbit's Series D delayed; acquisition talks deprioritized. Relationship continues as commercial partnership.
- **2028-02-28** | Contract renewal negotiations begin for Year 2. Orbit pushes for volume-based pricing tied to asset count growth.

_facts.name: Orbit Systems
_facts.description: Supply chain visibility platform; Anchor's first six-figure contract signed 2026-Q4
